title: Acoridium kingii
intro: Acoridium kingii, also known as the King's Walking Fern, is a rare and endangered species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical rainforests of the Philippines. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat. The bioactive constituents of Acoridium kingii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Acoridium kingii include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Acoridium kingii is classified as a species within the genus Acoridium. It belongs to the family Asparagaceae, which is a group of flowering plants. Acoridium kingii is further classified as an epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants for support. The species Acoridium kingii is native to the tropical regions of Asia and is often found in humid environments. Acoridium kingii's classification is subject to revision as botanical taxonomy continues to evolve.

Its roots have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism.
